Spinflint  

a rare creature
  • View
  • History
  • Media
  • Discuss
  • Tools
    • Main page
    • Print this page
    • What links here
    • Recent changes
    • Wiki search


Uploaded December 24th, 2016 by Severrian

  • This mob spawns at the middle gorge near the T and north of the eye/throne room.

Categories: Templatized | EverQuest
This page last modified 2020-06-29 04:52:09.

Level: 12
Expansion:
NPC Added: 0000-00-00 00:00:00
NPC Last Updated: 2019-06-20 10:45:30
Known Loot:
Whirling Agate
Whirling Amber
Whirling Bloodstone
Whirling Carnelian
Whirling Crystal
Whirling Hematite
Whirling Jasper
Whirling Lapis Lazuli
Whirling Malachite
Whirling Onyx
Whirling Quartz
Whirling Turquoise

Known Habitats:
  Gorge of King Xorbb (Beholder's Maze)
Quests:
  Hunter of The Gorge of King Xorbb
  Renni's Rock Collection

Report a correction

« Previous 1 2 3 Next »
Post Comment
Spawn
QuoteReply
# Jun 25 2020 at 10:49 AM Rating: Excellent
YoduhAgnarr
Sage
90 posts
Today on Aradune server he popped and was north of the eye/throne room. Nothing special, took about 2 hours and was the first named I saw in the zone when starting King camp.
▲ Page top
Hunter Spawn
QuoteReply
# Jul 22 2019 at 11:24 PM Rating: Good
lymark
Sage
69 posts
I did as subject [some information]. I had a ranger on East Karana zone line killing and Wizard with a flux staff. I spent about 2 - 2.5 hours. Spinflint appeared in the middle gorge near the T. Also, I have noticed something during some of my pulls with the wizard. If you grab a bunch of the mobs.

I mean most of the zone and just wait there and let the beat you up, give them about a minute or two,
the other mobs start to spawn as well.

The one night I grabbed mostly everything, letting the mobs beat on me for about 2 or 3 minutes.
Then King Xorbb, Yymp the Infernal, and Brahhm came out to play.

Also, I had my ranger snare Spinflint and then grabbed my wizard to meet up with my ranger. Then I was sending
tells if others want to join in for the flag. No one did. But in the process of waiting, Yymp the Infernal appeared and
started beating on my ranger.

Please reply if this is a conformation because I have not seen any comments concerning this kind of stratagy to get
the hunters to spawn.
▲ Page top
some information
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2017 at 2:45 AM Rating: Good
Shuraz
Sage
1,086 posts
Got this mob to spawn, no idea on a ph. I had been killing everything that walks by runny eye entrance. Once this guy spawned, I followed him for a while. His path was from approx. 1000 feet west of runny eye ( along the northern path) to runny eye, then south east passing by the karana fork to head northwest all the way past Sellernee and then east passing Brellgour turn - it did not turn towards Brellgur, but did stop just past that intersection. Then it returned the way it came until the karana fork, it went south partway, stopped for a moment, then back north, turned east and this time made the 2nd left (not towards the eyes, but the other dead end path) and stopped halfway, waited a moment, turned around and went back to where it started.

oh yeah edit - he was non-kos. and yes no faction hits as posted earlier

Edited, Aug 15th 2017 4:48am by Shuraz
▲ Page top
No Faction.
QuoteReply

snailish
4,580 posts
He was pathing on the east side today (towards the dead end with the wizard groundspawn item).

I have been in the Gorge a lot. You don't see him often.

# Jun 24 2006 at 1:02 AM Rating: Decent

626 posts
Just killed him. He was floating along one of the northwestern intersections of the Gorge (LOC: POS 900, NEG 75).


Loot: Whirling Turquoise

sitting and spawning
QuoteReply
# Nov 26 2005 at 12:50 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362939651
309 posts
sit up by the dark elf quest npc brellgour lynx. I did this for 21+hours in the last week. Skinflint popped once.

A good use of your time. I killed everything and repeatedly wiped the zone when no pops were at the intersection with the dark elf.

Funny enough, I got him on the same day as my first Xorbb. He was pathing up from the EK zone line, where I had been clearing minor muddites to trigger Xorbb. Dropped the Whirling Lapis Lazuli.

This is what works for me
QuoteReply
# May 04 2004 at 5:00 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591751742688
16 posts
I run around killing muddite elders & minor. I use a pet and have it kill them as well. After doing this for awhile either Spinflint or Qlei will pop. I've been here 3 days & doing this got Spinflint to pop twice & same for Qlei. Good luck all.

Found him the other night

Dropped the lapis
was green at 36

And it was the firiona vie server

I FOUND SPINFLINT!
QuoteReply
# Oct 25 2003 at 9:27 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592256343354
View User Forum
26 posts
I found Spinflint! He's at 185.20, -833.31, 63.38. It appears that he has spawned inside the walls of the mountain. I was running through the gorge this morning, checking track as usual, and there he was! However, I could not find him, and finally located him inside the wall at that location. I have submitted a bug report on it.
▲ Page top
RE: I FOUND SPINFLINT!
QuoteReply
# Mar 26 2004 at 10:01 AM Rating: Good
DrexelNecromancer
Scholar
166 posts
I wonder about this NOT being a bug. To me, it makes sense that a rock-based creature might very well have the ability to go through/spawn inside/move around in rock walls. It might very well be that SoE designed it that way. Having never seen him in the gorge, I'll now go in with a tracker and give it a try. Thanks for coordinates, though maybe he's not a static spawn?

Drexxell
Wayfarer of Veeshan
▲ Page top
RE: I FOUND SPINFLINT!
QuoteReply
# Oct 26 2004 at 8:37 PM Rating: Decent
Kyrros
Sage
164 posts
Trust me, this is not some roleplaying thing SoE did... its a bug.

Lavastorm Mountains also had the same problem with MoB's spawning in the geometry (as a result of some tinkering they did with spawn points right before they implemented the new engine). A few months ago I noticed in a patch that Lavastorm zone files had been re-downloaded... so I immediately ran over to the zone to find that the MoB spawns had indeed been fixed.

I hunt in Runnyeye regularly collecting Drops and Words/Pages - I was in the Gorge and noticed in the last month or so that mony more MoBs appear to be wandering the gorge now instead of being stuck in the walls...

Yay! No more AoE'ing myself :P
____________________________
Kyrros <Knights Of Insomnia> - Bristlebane
Guild Leader and Guild Historian
Human Veteran Ranger and Warder of Tunare for 70 80 85 90 seasons
Knights of Insomnia Homepage - Hosted by Guildportal.com
http://www.guildportal.com/Guild.aspx?GuildID=2542&TabID=16569
▲ Page top
I think I know why he's so rare
QuoteReply
# Aug 24 2003 at 4:10 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257648528
56 posts
Have been trying to complete Renni's jewel collection with a 44 Ranger. I've been plowing through the Gorge, killing everything that moves (and some things that don't).

In the process I discovered two things:

1. It's possible for an Evil Eye to spawn outside of the area with the altar. It appears to spawn in the northernmost passage. I'm wondering if this is a placeholder for the King.

2. Roughly half of the creatures in this zone are unkillable because they have spawned inside the walls. As a Ranger with tracking I can pinpoint them. In fact I 'watched' (on track) a goblin veteran spawn in the north wall of the zone several times. Each time it despawned ('you have lost your target') within a minute or so, probably because it was completely outside the zone. But the mobs in the walls apparently don't despawn.

Why is this significant? Because if one of the mobs in the walls, who can't be targeted or attacked and thus can't be killed, is a PH for Spinflint, he will never spawn.

Another reason I think some of these mobs are so 'rare' is because the muddites are their placeholders, and very few people like to kill the muddites because of their blindness attack and their crappy loot (unless you're a potter).

May have to take a Bard in there and play an AoE damage song to see if I can coax any of these guys out of their cozy walls.
▲ Page top
RE: I think I know why he's so rare
QuoteReply
# Oct 19 2003 at 2:26 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1711930564480
3 posts
I know that a PBAoE will coax the muddites, etc. out of the wall. I have done it many times hunting for heavy clay.

Laurrill Nimgulas
Wizard 55
Saryrn Server

# Jan 01 2003 at 12:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361986273
2 posts
saw this one while running through the gorge. I was on a CR and couldn't summon a pet to attack and MOB resisted almost all of my fire line of spells. Those not resisted were only 1/4 damage at most. I didn't see him spawn but he was wandering from the RE end towards EK zone. So much for a 22 mage meleeing this MOB :)

#REDACTED, Posted: May 30 2002 at 1:19 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) As my experiance as a DM for D&D serves me right, these are just a Take-Off from the Iuon/Ioun stones which float around a persons head, colored and shaped based on thier purpose.
D&D Item..?

he was up after a patch about a month ago
was farming the eyes at the time
kill him drop a Whriling Diamond

couple weeks later
killed him again
drop diamond again
then again yesterday
i killed him got a whriling BD
+10svall +30mana Headslot no-drop
i killed him today got a
+3int head
